empty.subtitle: There are no habits for today.
empty.title: No habits

terms.s1-intro: Dooit collects data related to your habits at two points: after completing the onboarding process and after completing the daily summary.
terms.s1-body: The collected data includes habit names, frequency of completion, and progress statistics. This data is used solely to analyze which habits users build most often and in what quantity. This analysis helps improve the app in line with users’ real needs.
terms.s2-body: All data is collected anonymously. Your username is not transmitted or stored outside your device. Habit data is labeled with a randomly generated identifier. Your data is not sold or shared with third parties.
terms.s3-body: You can permanently delete all your data at any time in the Settings tab. The data will be removed both from your device and from the Dooit database. Data deletion is irreversible.
terms.s4-body: Dooit is free to use. The only official version is available on Google Play. The source code is publicly available under the CC BY-NC 4.0 license — you are free to view and use it for non-commercial purposes, but commercial use is strictly prohibited.

message.delete-habit: Are you sure to remove the habit:
message.delete-execution: Are you sure to remove this execution?
message.delete-data: Are you sure? This will permanently delete all your habits, history, and account data.
message.change-goal:
Do you want to change the goal?
By default, it is set as the number of repetitions to be done within 4 weeks.
message.goal-reached: You've reached your goal! Want to push further and increase the limit to build an even stronger habit?
message.contact: Fill out the form to contact the app's author.
message.support:
Hi, {{userName}}!

I’m building Dooit on my own, with strong belief that it can truly improve everyday life. Every line of code was written to help you build habits that make a real difference.

If you feel that Dooit has supported you in some way, I’d appreciate it a lot if you contributed to its development - even with a virtual coffee. It’s a sign for me that what I’m doing matters.

~ Mikołaj Bryksa

contact.rate-limit: You've reached the daily limit (3 messages).

addStep.habitName: What do you want to do regularly?
addStep.repeatDays: On what days you want to do it?
addStep.repeatHours: What time do you want to do it?

tip.onboarding-habit-mindset: Think of a habit not as a big task, but as the first small step it requires. Instead of overwhelming yourself with the idea of drinking 3 liters of water every day, just focus from time to time on pouring a glass of water.
tip.onboarding-same-time: Habits are easiest to build when you repeat them at the same time on the same days. Over time, you’ll start to naturally feel what action to take at that moment. In the beginning, set only a few repetitions so it’s easy to start, and increase the intensity later.
tip.now-repetitions-matter: In Dooit, only repetitions matter. You don’t need to worry about the result you achieve today while doing a task. You’re not tracking calories, liters, kilograms, laps, or minutes. The goal is simply to learn how to consistently come back to the planned activity.
tip.now-first-habit: Below you see what matters most to you right now, {{userName}}. Focus only on that, don’t think about other tasks to check off. Then mark whether you completed it and move on. This is how real change happens.
tip.summary-daily-recap: After completing all habits planned for the day, Dooit will summarize how many repetitions you completed and provide a short analysis of your day. It will highlight what went best and what was the most challenging.
tip.habits-edit-and-goal: You can edit any habit at any time. The default goal is the number of repetitions calculated for 28 days, which is usually enough to build a habit. You can always increase the goal to strengthen your new routine even more!
tip.settings-overview: Here you can update your name used in daily summaries, change the app language, switch between dark and light mode, and enable or disable notifications. If you want to start over, you’ll find an option at the bottom to delete all your data.

step2.which-habits: which habits you want to build

step3.accept-default: or keep the default repetition settings

start.subtitle: Hi, {{userName}}
start.title: Let’s get started
start.text:
For each habit,
just make one choice:
do it or skip it.

lastChance:
Count 5, 4, 3, 2, 1 and take action!

Mel Robbins shows that a quick countdown forces your brain to act immediately, before excuses can take over.

Take at least one small step today.

BJ Fogg shows that even minimal actions build habits and increase your chances of success in daily life.

Imagine how your future self will thank you.

Hal Hershfield shows that visualizing the consequences of your decisions increases the satisfaction of taking action.
done: One good habit leads to another.
Another brick in the wall of success.
Each action shapes who you become.
You’re building the best version of you.
Great choice, your routine is working.
skipped: One slip doesn’t erase your progress.
Learn from it, then move forward.
It’s a stumble, not a failure.
Your habit is stronger than this moment.
One skip is not the end.
It’s just a break, not a setback.
It’s just a pause, your journey continues.
intro: Another day behind you, {{userName}}. Every time you open Dooit is a step in the right direction.
Great to see you back again, {{userName}}. Consistency is the key to building lasting habits.
Habits are built day by day, {{userName}}. That’s why every day and every decision counts.
I’m really glad Dooit is part of your daily routine, {{userName}}. Consistency is the secret to lasting change.
The more often you come back, {{userName}}, the faster you’ll build your habits and change your life.
praise: Great job staying consistent with the habit '{{bestHabit}}'! You nailed it today.
It looks like you're making great progress with the habit '{{bestHabit}}'. You're doing really well!
Can you already feel the effects of repeating the habit '{{bestHabit}}'? You're on the right track to making it life-changing.
I'm really impressed by your consistency with the habit '{{bestHabit}}'. Keep up the great work!
The habit '{{bestHabit}}' is your biggest win today. Keep it going!
tip: It looks like something is blocking the habit '{{worstHabit}}'. Maybe try simplifying the first step?
Is the habit '{{worstHabit}}' scheduled at the right time of day? Maybe try a different time?
With the habit '{{worstHabit}}', you might be starting too big. What would the simplest version look like?
Can you link the habit '{{worstHabit}}' to something you already do regularly? It’s easier to stack habits together.
If the habit '{{worstHabit}}' isn’t going as planned, it’s just a signal to adjust your strategy — not a failure.

summary.subtitle: All done today!
summary.title: Daily summary
summary.no-actions: Your progress summary will appear here once you complete at least one repetition of your chosen habit.

The error has been reported automatically.
I am sorry for the inconvenience.
